title: Time Picker
description: Time Picker is a standalone wheel-based selector for hours and minutes. It supports 24 hour and 12 hour presentation, minute step intervals, size variants, inline layout, labels, and disabled state.
intro: Use `ui-time-picker` when you need the time selection primitive directly, outside of the shared field wrapper. It works well in drawers, scheduling panels, compact operational rows, and custom overlays where a full `ui-time` field would add unnecessary chrome.

featureSections.title: Basic wheel selection
featureSections.description: The basic picker exposes two wheels and emits a normalized `HH:mm` string. This is the right baseline when the surrounding layout already provides context for what the time means.

featureSections.title: Minute step and 12h or 24h format
featureSections.description: Step intervals control how operational the picker feels, while 12 hour mode adds explicit AM or PM choice. Show both together because they are usually configured as one product decision.

featureSections.title: Size and inline layout
featureSections.description: Use size to match surrounding density and inline mode when the picker has to live inside tighter rows, tool panels, or compact embedded surfaces.

featureSections.title: Disabled and preset states
featureSections.description: State handling matters more than chrome in real use. Show disabled and prefilled variants clearly so teams understand how the picker behaves when a slot is locked or inherited from workflow defaults.

featureSections.title: Inline schedule row
featureSections.description: A good standalone picker example should show it in a compact operational row rather than only as an isolated widget. Inline start and end slots are a common real pattern.

featureSections.title: Scheduling panel composition
featureSections.description: More advanced examples should combine the picker with status context, actions, and supporting details. This is closer to how product teams actually embed standalone time selection.

accessibility.title: Wheel and option semantics
accessibility.body:
`ui-time-picker` renders separate hour and minute listboxes with option buttons, plus an AM or PM radio group in 12 hour mode.

| Part | Semantics |
| --- | --- |
| hour wheel | `role="listbox"` with option buttons |
| minute wheel | `role="listbox"` with option buttons |
| 12 hour selector | radio button group for `AM` or `PM` |
| disabled state | wheels and meridiem controls become non-interactive |

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
Keyboard support follows the wheel interaction model.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` | moves between hour wheel, minute wheel, and AM or PM group when present |
| `ArrowUp` / `ArrowDown` | moves by one option in the focused wheel |
| `PageUp` / `PageDown` | jumps by larger increments in the focused wheel |
| click or button activation | selects a visible option from the wheel |

accessibility.title: Labels and context
accessibility.body: Because this component is often embedded directly into custom panels, give it surrounding context. Use `showLabel` and `label` when the picker stands on its own, or place it near explicit section text so screen reader users understand whether the slot controls a start time, end time, handoff, or deadline.

apiSections.title: TimePickerComponent - ui-time-picker
apiSections.lead: Standalone wheel-based time selector for direct embedding inside custom layouts, drawers, dialogs, and composite scheduling surfaces.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Normalized selected time string such as `09:30`.
Minute or second step used to build the minute wheel cadence.
Density token for wheel height and option size.
Switches between 24 hour and 12 hour presentation.
Reduces surrounding chrome for denser embedded layouts.
Shows a visible label above or beside the picker layout.
Visible label text when `showLabel` is enabled.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits the normalized `HH:mm` value after wheel or meridiem selection.

blocks.title: Behavior notes
rows: Generated from the resolved step cadence, normalized to the nearest available option.
Keeps stored values normalized as 24 hour `HH:mm` while presenting `AM` or `PM` in the UI.
Supports pointer dragging, mouse wheel, click selection, and keyboard navigation on each wheel.

blocks.title: Notes
paragraphs: Use `ui-time-picker` directly when the page already provides its own form framing or when a wheel-based embedded picker is the intended interaction.
If you need a shared field shell, validation, helper text, and mobile-native fallback, prefer `ui-time` instead of wiring those pieces manually around this primitive.
